comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

I reviewed AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters#224691 and I'm ready to handle it.
- My read: this is primarily a UI/UX task tied to "www.lide.cz".
- I will focus on these issue-specific points first: www, lide, url.
- First I will reproduce and confirm expected behavior for the UI/UX path.
- Then I will implement the fix with targeted tests and keep scope tight (good confidence).
- I will share a working PR update fast (24h for first patch, 48h for final polish) with verification notes.
If this approach works for you, I can start now.

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.title: www.youtube.com

labels.name: T: Anti Adblock Script
labels.description: The site detects ad blocking

labels.name: NSFW
labels.description: Nudes and porn sites

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# ATTENTION

> [!NOTE]  
> YouTube changes their detection scripts regularly. That means that sometime you can face the detection message.
> If all the following instructions do not work, please **email filters@adguard.com** or wait until any update is available. 

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use another adblocker in the same time because using another adblocker can delay/lags the YouTube loading (e.g., AdBlock Plus and AdBlock)[^1] OR interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use an userscript related to the bypassing YouTube detection message like [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing).
> Recently, YouTube anti-adblock script added infinite dummy advertisements to try to neutralize the userscript. ( [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing#264](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing/issues/264) )

> [!WARNING]
> Please check that there no another web browser extension that has adblocking feature is enabled on your web browser. It can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> Saving memory feature of your web browser or sleep feature of your operating system can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.
> In this case, please re-enable AdGuard's filtering by toggling the protection toggle.
> Following the instructions can be needed.

### The instructions
1. Force to update the filters by clicking the reload button in AdGuard Browser Extension/AdGuard mobile or update tray menu in your system tray. [^2]
2. Reload the YouTube webpage without cache by pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd> [^3].
3. Clear cache, cookies and site data of your web browser.
4. Re-open your web browser completely (checking your system monitor[^4] will be better).

[^1]: https://gitlab.com/adblockinc/ext/adblockplus/adblockplusui/-/issues/1576
[^2]: [AdGuard for Windows](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-windows/overview/#tray-menu); [AdGuard for Mac](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-mac/overview/#updates-check); 
[^3]: On macOS, <kbd>Command</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
[^4]: Task Manager on Windows, System Monitor on GNOME Shell, KDE System Guard on KDE Plasma or Activity Monitor on macOS.

comment.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=m6O7XJZWfuQ&list=RDm6O7XJZWfuQ&start_radio=1](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv%3Dm6O7XJZWfuQ%26list%3DRDm6O7XJZWfuQ%26start_radio%3D1) ### Comment > Ever since I update Adguard for Safari and it became Adguard Mini I've experienced a significant lack of ad protection when it comes to Youtube videos. While I understand counteracting Youtube's anti-adblock attempts is difficult, it can't be ignored that the previous version (Adguard for Safari) was much more successful in blocking Youtube's ads even with the occasional glitches (i.e. the "experiencing interruptions?" pop-up, the "this content isn't available" page, the lack of playback speed options until refreshed), and that perhaps I should not have updated considering Adguard Mini's ineffectiveness at its main job. > > This has happened at least half a dozen times since updating the other day and the ads usually appear after using Youtube's search function and clicking on one of the videos. Please fix this issue. ``` Username: @DisappointedAdguardVeteran ``` ### Screenshots

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.chatgpt.com](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.chatgpt.com) ### Comment > Cannot upload any screenshots to ChatGPT with AdGuard Home enabled. See red banner at top of first screenshot. When disabled, screenshots upload fine, see second screenshot for example. Checked Query Log and couldn’t find anything blocked having to do with ChatGPT. ChatGPT itself indicated that files.oaiusercontent.com needs to be allowed but there were previous instances of it being allowed, and it doesn't show up in the logs at al during these failures. ``` Username: keyadash@gmail.com ``` ### Screenshots

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
> website has very low traffic Unfortunately, the reported issue does not satisfy our [filters policy](https://kb.adguard.com/en/general/adguard-filter-policy).

@ghajini 

This annoyance is shown on **_any_** podcast on Podbean, not just on the example I gave. 
Podbean is one of the world's most popular podcast hosting services. I don't think it's a low traffic website at all.

To be clear: I think it should be added as a annoyance to **AdGuard Mobile App Banners filter**. (not to **AdGuard Mobile Ads filter**).
Isn't it the purpose of **AdGuard Mobile App Banners filter** ?

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.wired.com/account/profile/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.wired.com%2Faccount%2Fprofile%2F) ### Comment > Parts of wired.com do not work correctly with AdGuard enabled. For this specific case, the account profile page is missing almost all of its content. Wired.com appears to be unable to get the user's subscription status when AdGuard is turned on.
